data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T10:13:10.842Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like Assert.StartsWith and Assert.EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.content: Quick note: MSTest doesn't have `Assert.StartsWith`/`Assert.EndsWith`. The correct class is **`StringAssert`** — e.g., `StringAssert.StartsWith(...)` and `StringAssert.EndsWith(...)`. I'll set up a working project using those.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to write MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically mention using `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods. Let me think about this carefully.

In MSTest, the standard assertion methods are on the `Assert` class. However, `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` are **not** standard MSTest methods. The standard MSTest `Assert` class has methods like `Assert.AreEqual`, `Assert.IsTrue`, `Assert.IsNotNull`, etc. 

For string-specific assertions, MSTest has `StringAssert` class with methods like:


- `StringAssert.StartsWith(string value, string substring)`
- `StringAssert.EndsWith(string value, string substring)`
- `StringAssert.Contains(string value, string substring)`
- `StringAssert.Matches(string value, Regex pattern)`

So `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` don't exist in MSTest. The correct methods are `StringAssert.StartsWith` and `StringAssert.EndsWith`. I should clarify this for the user and write the tests using the proper `StringAssert` methods instead. Now I need to set up the test project structure and create the actual test cases.

data.content: All 28 tests pass. Let me view the final test file:

**All 28 tests pass.** Key correction: MSTest uses the **`StringAssert`** class (not `Assert`) for string-specific assertions:

| Method | Example |
|---|---|
| `StringAssert.StartsWith` | Greeting starts with `"Dear "` |
| `StringAssert.EndsWith` | Greeting ends with `","` |
| `StringAssert.Contains` | Phone number contains `"-"` |
| `StringAssert.Matches` | Slug matches `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$` |

The tests cover all four methods — happy paths, edge cases, format validation, and exception handling — across `FormatPhoneNumber`, `BuildGreeting`, `GenerateSlug`, and `FormatCurrency`.
